mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary pressures, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series signals, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in >55% of cases during expansionary periods, or the mechanism reverses direction during inflationary spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate labor market tightening and rising household income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict short-term equity direction, with a 5-day forward return accuracy below 52% over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 215.0, inflationary pressures force monetary authorities to restrict liquidity, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, or the inflation threshold loses predictive power after regime shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and consumption signal shows no incremental predictive value over single indicators, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of aligned periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during late-cycle phases signal capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative short-term returns during late-cycle periods, with a 5-day forward return accuracy below 48% over rolling 3-year windows.
